articles.title: Ram Temple 'donation theft': Akhilesh and Yogi trade barbs

Yadav accused the BJP of running a business under the guise of religion, prioritizing money over faith. Meanwhile, Ayodhya police raided the homes of those arrested in the alleged donation embezzlement case, as political parties traded barbs over the issue.

articles.title: Trinamool files four fresh complaints against rebel faction
articles.description: The Trinamool Congress is facing an internal crisis as the Mamata Banerjee faction filed multiple police complaints against dissident leader Ritabrata Banerjee and his group. Allegations include unauthorized use of the party's logo and impersonating party leadership, notably naming Arup Roy as chairperson. The complaints also question the funding behind the dissident faction's activities, hinting at a deeper power struggle within the party.

articles.title: J&K outsourcing recruitment row: NC rebuts PDP's 'backdoor jobs' charge
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir's ruling National Conference and opposition PDP are locked in a bitter dispute over recruitment via outsourcing. The PDP accuses the NC of backdoor appointments, benefiting outsiders and party loyalists, while the NC claims the PDP initiated the outsourcing process during its previous coalition. Both parties accuse each other of advancing the BJP's agenda and engaging in corrupt practices.

articles.title: Bengal set to table UCC, public safety bills today in Assembly
articles.description: West Bengal is set to introduce a Uniform Civil Code Bill, aiming to replace religious personal laws with a single set for all citizens, promoting equality and fulfilling constitutional directives. The BJP has long advocated for this, with Union Home Minister Amit Shah previously pledging its introduction. Notably, Scheduled Tribes will be exempt from the UCC.

articles.description: Tech leaders like Peter Diamandis and Larry Ellison are championing a future of 'radical transparency' enabled by a vast network of sensors. They argue that constant monitoring, from home cameras to orbiting satellites, will foster better human behaviour as 'no one can hide.'

articles.title: Apple seeks US approval to buy chips from blacklisted Chinese company
articles.description: Reports suggest that Apple is actively reaching out to the Trump administration in an effort to source memory chips from ChangXin Memory Technologies, a Chinese firm currently banned by the Pentagon. This initiative aims to combat the financial burden caused by rising chip costs, a prevalent issue for various U.S. tech companies.

articles.title: SpaceX set to join Nasdaq 100, paving way for wave of passive buying
articles.description: SpaceX is set to join the Nasdaq 100 index on July 7, a move expected to attract billions in passive investment. This inclusion follows Nasdaq's relaxed entry criteria, making it easier for companies like SpaceX, despite recent losses, to enter major indices. While some see it as a positive sign of demand, others, like Morningstar, express concerns about the stock's valuation. S&P 500, however, maintains stricter entry rules for SpaceX.

articles.description: There are architectural flaws in chatbot interactions that refuse to go away and make it difficult to trust them.

articles.title: West Asia conflict may delay India's chip projects
articles.description: India's ambitious semiconductor projects, including Tata Electronics' Dholera fab, face potential delays due to the West Asia conflict. Logistical snags have disrupted the supply of crucial speciality gases, chemicals, and metals, leading to concerns about cost overruns and extended timelines. While some believe disruptions have eased, experts warn of significant pushes to production schedules, impacting both construction and operational phases.

articles.title: 25% of Indian tech services firms have moved AI experiments into production level: Nasscom
articles.description: ‘Indian IT is currently generating up to $12 billion in AI services revenue, with more than 2 million professionals skilled in AI and 100,000 to 200,000 trained in advanced AI capabilities’

articles.description: Centre’s clearances for felling nearly 2.8 million trees, coupled with large-scale infrastructure in hill regions, could destabilise fragile mountain ecosystems, says the chairman of the civil society group

articles.title: OpenAI weighs IPO in 2027 after expected Anthropic public debut
articles.description: AI giant OpenAI is reportedly eyeing a 2027 initial public offering, potentially after rival Anthropic. Both companies have confidentially filed for an IPO, with Anthropic possibly listing as early as October. Recent tech market volatility has influenced OpenAI's timing, though deliberations are ongoing. This move aims to secure public market funding for substantial AI infrastructure investments.

articles.title: Bell and Trott lent me their bats; and I said, my gosh, I would like to make a comeback: Dennis Amiss
articles.description: The 83-year-old Englishman has had a significant influence on cricket’s evolution, going where no man had gone before in ODIs and mainstreaming protective headgear. In this interview, he talks about living through a game-changing era, appreciating modern cricket, making big hundreds, and being tempted to return when he first wielded a modern bat

US stocks fall as report of OpenAI IPO delay causes jitters
articles.description: Wall Street stocks fell on Friday, capping a roller-coaster week for tech stocks as traders fretted that valuations may be entering bubble territory. This was in part caused by Elon Musk's rocket company SpaceX seeing its valuation drop from its IPO-day highs this week, pointing to a rockier landing zone for blockbuster public offerings on Wall Street.

articles.description: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.

articles.description: Byju's operated in more than 21 countries at one point and became ‌popular during ⁠the COVID-19 ⁠pandemic by offering online courses. But its fortunes changed in early 2023 when the high-profile ​dispute with its U.S.-based lenders began.

articles.title: Uber India head Prabhjeet Singh quits, to join as OpenAI MD
articles.description: Uber India's head, Prabhjeet Singh, is departing after an 11-year tenure to join OpenAI as its India MD. Singh, led Uber's significant growth and strategic shifts, including expanding premium services and launching electric mobility. His departure comes as India is poised to become Uber's largest market globally.

articles.title: Antarctic sea squirt could help unlock a new skin cancer treatment, scientists say
Samsung readies $648 billion bet, report says, as AI boom reshapes South Korea
‘Developers are moving from writing code to orchestrating AI agents’: AWS’s Jeff Barr
EU tech chief Virkkunen holds call with Apple CEO on Monday, Commission says
articles.description: The European Union's top tech official, Virkkunen, is scheduled for an urgent call with Apple's CEO on Monday. This high-level meeting, confirmed by the Commission, likely addresses ongoing digital regulations and tech policy discussions between the EU and the prominent technology giant.

articles.title: Drone startup Elroy Air to list on Nasdaq via $1 billion SPAC deal
articles.description: Cargo drone startup Elroy Air is set to go public in the US through a $1 billion merger with blank-check firm Columbus Circle Capital Corp II. This strategic move, expected to yield at least $165 million, will fuel Elroy Air's development of autonomous heavy-cargo drones for defence and logistics.

articles.title: Apple supplier Tata Electronics tightens internal controls after data breach
articles.description: Tata Electronics is investigating a massive data leak on the dark web, reportedly exposing thousands of secret client files from Apple and Tesla. The company has restricted internal system access, hired a global consultant for a forensic audit, and informed authorities and clients. Apple's security team is actively collaborating with Tata on mitigation efforts following this significant cybersecurity incident.

articles.title: Swatch claiming $170 million in damages against Samsung over trademark infringement: FT
articles.description: Swiss watchmaker Swatch is demanding $170 million from Samsung, alleging the tech giant facilitated digital replicas of Swatch's iconic watch designs on its smartwatches. A British court previously found Samsung liable for trademark infringement. This ruling, stemming from a 2019 lawsuit, could pave the way for a similar case in the US.

articles.title: Google announces new Finance app, expands portfolio tracking and market intelligence
Buyers less sceptical of Lava phones, says MD Sunil Raina
articles.description: Lava is quite happy serving in the sub-30k price category due to volume game and thinks that anything beyond that is vanity

articles.title: Lava launches Smart 4 Plus for budget buyers in India
articles.description: Lava Smart 4 Plus runs on Unisoc 9863A processor with 4 GB RAM and 64 GB storage. It operates on Android 15 Go version and will get 2 years of assured security updates

articles.title: Science of deception: Why our brain loves to lie
articles.description: Let’s explore how evolution taught our brains to deceive in order to avoid conflicts.

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.description: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.

articles.title: Amazon open to acquisitions in India, says CEO Andy Jassy
articles.description: Asked whether Amazon would consider acquisitions in the quick commerce space and whether it might invest in homegrown AI companies such as Sarvam AI or Emergent Labs as part of India's sovereign AI push, the CEO said the company would evaluate potential targets based on their technology, customer experience, and talent.

articles.title: Uniform Civil Code will be implemented in Bengal, CM Suvendu Adhikari says
articles.description: Speaking to the media, Adhikari said a committee is constituted under the leadership of a sitting inquiry authority, adding that details of the UCC would be shared in the Assembly on Monday.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh wants the US Fed to speak less—but has he really thought this idea through?
articles.description: The new US Federal Reserve chair is shaking things up, but not all these changes bode well. Eliminating forward guidance has merit, but obscuring the central bank’s monetary policy reaction function could unsettle markets and also get in the way of policy transmission.

articles.title: Australia pledges tougher enforcement of social media ban for teens
articles.description: Australia is strengthening laws to ban social media for under-16s, despite initial challenges in enforcement. Prime Minister Albanese vows to "bullet-proof" legislation as the government prepares legal action against platforms for failing to verify ages. While studies show many teens still access platforms, experts note a shift in parental attitudes and social norms around early social media use, suggesting a gradual impact.

articles.title: Shashi Tharoor proposes dual passport-Aadhaar document policy to end citizenship proof row
articles.description: While acknowledging that the government's position is rooted in Section 20 of the Passports Act, 1967, which permits passports to be issued to non-citizens in limited public-interest circumstances, Tharoor argued that the distinction holds little meaning for ordinary citizens.

articles.title: Indian electronics manufacturing: the sector must bet on design capability at this phase of its evolution
articles.description: Design is the layer of manufacturing that will entrench the sector deeper in global supply chains. A foundation was laid. Now let’s push for a product-design revolution. Value escalation lies in technology ownership and creation
